A–I
Coverage
Full

EN
Language
Module 2
Exploring AI Use Cases and Applications
Section / Root
Subsection
Concept / Term
Advantage / Use
Exception / Warning
Risk / Cost
AWS Service / Note
A
AI Fundamentals Recap
A
A. AI Fundamentals Recap │ ├── A1. Hierarchy │ ├── AI ⊃ ML ⊃ DL ⊃ Generative AI — nested, not competing │ ├── Generative AI creates · Traditional ML analyzes / predicts │ └── Distinction recurs across every industry use case in this module │ └── A2. Generative AI Content Types ├── Text — conversations, summaries, articles, reports ├── Visual — images, video, illustrations, product renders ├── Audio — music, speech synthesis, voiceovers, sound effects └── Code — program generation, function writing, code review
B
Real-World AI Use Cases Across Industries
B
B. Real-World AI Use Cases Across Industries │ ├── B1. Business Value Mechanisms │ ├── Automation — replace repetitive manual work │ ├── Optimization — improve decisions beyond human capacity │ └── Personalization — tailor experiences at scale │ └── B2. Industry Breakdown ├── Media & Entertainment │ ├── Content Generation — scripts, stories, automation │ ├── Virtual Reality — immersive AI-generated environments │ └── News Generation — automatic article summaries ├── Retail │ ├── Review Summaries — condensed product insights │ ├── Pricing Optimization — dynamic margin maximization │ ├── Virtual Try-ons — simulate fitting without physical contact │ └── Store Layout Optimization — improve shopping flow ├── Healthcare │ ├── Clinical Documentation — auto-generate clinical notes │ │ └── AWS HealthScribe — provider-patient conversation → structured notes │ ├── Personalized Medicine — tailor treatments to individual profiles │ └── Medical Imaging Enhancement — improve diagnostic accuracy ├── Life Sciences │ ├── Drug Discovery — generate and evaluate molecular structures │ ├── Protein Folding Prediction — predict 3D protein structures │ └── Synthetic Biology — engineer biological systems computationally ├── Financial Services │ ├── Fraud Detection — identify anomalous transactions in real time │ ├── Portfolio Management — simulate market scenarios for risk assessment │ └── Debt Collection Optimization — improve strategy and timing └── Manufacturing ├── Predictive Maintenance — detect failure before it occurs ├── Process Optimization — improve throughput and efficiency ├── Product Design Generation — generate designs within constraints └── Material Science — discover new materials computationally
C
Core AI Application Domains
C
C. Core AI Application Domains │ ├── C1. Computer Vision (CV) │ ├── Definition — enable machines to interpret images and video │ ├── Tasks (in order of granularity) │ │ ├── Image Classification — assign a label to the whole image │ │ ├── Object Detection — locate + label multiple objects with bounding boxes │ │ └── Segmentation — classify every pixel to its object (most granular) │ ├── Use Cases │ │ ├── Autonomous Driving — real-time lane, pedestrian, sign detection │ │ ├── Healthcare Imaging — radiology and pathology diagnosis accuracy │ │ └── Public Safety — facial recognition, surveillance │ └── AWS: Amazon Rekognition │ ├── C2. Natural Language Processing (NLP) │ ├── Definition — enable machines to understand and generate human language │ ├── Tasks │ │ ├── Text Classification — categorize documents by topic or intent │ │ ├── Sentiment Analysis — detect positive / negative / neutral tone │ │ ├── Translation — convert text between languages │ │ └── Text Generation — produce coherent language continuations │ ├── Use Cases │ │ ├── Insurance — extract policy clauses and data │ │ ├── Telecom — personalized product recommendations │ │ └── Education — chatbot Q&A for student support │ └── AWS: Amazon Comprehend · Amazon Translate · Amazon Lex │ ├── C3. Intelligent Document Processing (IDP) │ ├── Definition — AI extraction of insights from unstructured documents │ ├── IDP = superset workflow applying NLP + CV to document problems │ ├── Functions │ │ ├── Data Extraction — pull values from forms, tables, and text │ │ ├── Classification — route documents to correct workflow by type │ │ └── Summarization — condense long documents to key points │ ├── Use Cases │ │ ├── Finance — loan application processing │ │ ├── Legal — contract review and clause extraction │ │ └── Healthcare — insurance claims and patient records │ └── AWS: Amazon Textract (OCR + form extraction) │ └── C4. Fraud Detection ├── Definition — identify anomalous / malicious patterns in data ├── ML approach: learns what normal looks like → flags statistical deviations ├── Catches novel fraud no rule anticipated — unlike rigid rule-based filters └── Use Cases ├── Finance — AML, transaction anomaly, credit card fraud ├── Retail — account takeover, return fraud, fake reviews └── Telecom — SIM fraud, subscription abuse, network intrusion
D
When to Use ML — and When Not To
D
D. When to Use ML — and When Not To │ └── D1. Decision Framework ├── USE ML: Complex Rules │ ├── Too many edge cases to define manually │ └── Example: spam filtering ├── USE ML: High Variability │ ├── Many interacting factors, non-linear relationships │ └── Example: credit risk scoring ├── USE ML: Large Scale │ ├── Massive datasets humans cannot manually process │ └── Example: real-time recommendation systems ├── SKIP ML: Simple Logic │ ├── Deterministic rules already exist and cover all cases │ └── Example: tax calculation ├── SKIP ML: Low Complexity │ ├── Predefined steps are sufficient │ └── Example: unit conversion (°C → °F) └── ML is not universally better — better only when rules cannot scale or be defined
E
ML Techniques and Their Use Cases
E
E. ML Techniques and Their Use Cases │ ├── E1. Supervised Learning │ ├── Input: labeled data (every example has a known correct output) │ ├── Goal: predict outputs for new, unseen inputs │ ├── Classification — categorical output (one of a fixed set of classes) │ │ ├── Fraud detection — fraud / not fraud │ │ ├── Image classification — cat / dog / bird │ │ ├── Customer churn prediction — will / will not churn │ │ └── Medical diagnostics — disease positive / negative │ └── Regression — continuous output (numeric value on a scale) │ ├── Weather forecasting — predict temperature │ ├── Market trend prediction — predict stock price movement │ ├── Life expectancy estimation │ └── Population growth modeling │ ├── E2. Unsupervised Learning │ ├── Input: unlabeled data — no correct answers provided │ ├── Goal: discover hidden structure the model creates itself │ ├── Clustering — group similar data without predefined categories │ │ ├── Customer segmentation │ │ ├── Targeted marketing │ │ └── Recommendation systems │ └── Dimensionality Reduction — reduce features, preserve structure │ ├── Data visualization — project to 2D / 3D │ ├── Compression │ └── Feature extraction │ └── E3. Reinforcement Learning ├── Agent — decision-maker that takes actions ├── Environment — space the agent interacts with ├── Reward — positive feedback for correct actions ├── Penalty — negative feedback for incorrect actions ├── Policy — learned strategy that maximizes cumulative reward ├── No labeled dataset — learns through trial and error └── AWS: DeepRacer — autonomous 1/18th-scale race car via RL
F
Capabilities of Generative AI
F
F. Capabilities of Generative AI │ └── F1. The Seven Capabilities ├── Adaptability — single FM flexes across tasks and domains without rebuilding ├── Responsiveness — real-time output generation (conversational, live) ├── Simplicity — automates complex workflows without specialized skill ├── Creativity — produces genuinely novel outputs, not template recombinations ├── Data Efficiency — adapts to new tasks with limited additional data (pretrained base) ├── Personalization — tailors outputs per user at scale — impossible for rule-based systems ├── Scalability — produces content volume no human team can match └── Core value: automation + creativity at scale — simultaneously
G
Challenges and Risks of Generative AI
G
G. Challenges and Risks of Generative AI │ ├── G1. The Seven Risks │ ├── Hallucinations — plausible but factually false outputs │ │ ├── Model predicts likely text, not correct text │ │ └── Primary exam-tested risk · Must always verify AI outputs │ ├── Toxicity — offensive / harmful / discriminatory outputs │ ├── Privacy Concerns — regeneration of personal data seen in training │ ├── Regulatory Violations — expose PII / PHI or generate non-compliant content │ ├── Social Risks — deepfakes, misinformation, brand reputation damage │ ├── Interpretability — hard to explain why a specific output was produced │ └── Nondeterminism — same input → different outputs across runs │ └── G2. Mitigation Strategies ├── Guardrails — filter / block harmful output categories before delivery ├── Verification / RAG — ground outputs in retrieved facts (primary hallucination fix) ├── Data Anonymization — remove / mask PII before training or prompting ├── Data Curation — remove harmful / biased content from training data ├── Model Testing — evaluate for bias, toxicity, accuracy before deployment ├── Monitoring — continuously detect inconsistencies and drift in production └── Security Measures — encryption, firewalls, access controls
H
Model Selection Factors
H
H. Model Selection Factors │ ├── H1. Five Core Factors │ ├── Model Type — task-capability alignment (first gate) │ ├── Performance — accuracy, reliability, benchmark scores │ ├── Constraints — compute (GPU/CPU/memory), data, deployment environment │ ├── Compliance — GDPR, HIPAA, ethics, org policies │ │ └── Hard gate — non-compliant models are disqualified regardless of accuracy │ └── Cost — size vs. speed vs. quality trade-off │ ├── Large Models — higher accuracy, higher cost per inference, slower │ └── Small Models — lower cost, faster latency, lower accuracy on complex tasks │ ├── H2. Foundation Model Examples │ ├── AI21 Jurassic-2 — text generation, summarization, Q&A │ ├── Amazon Titan — embeddings, semantic search, Q&A (AWS-native) │ ├── Anthropic Claude — chat, reasoning, coding, long context │ ├── Stable Diffusion — image generation from text prompts │ ├── Cohere Command — NLP tasks (classification, summarization) │ └── Meta Llama — general text processing, open-weight, fine-tuning base │ └── H3. Selection Principle └── Model selection = task + cost + performance balance · No universal winner
I
Business Metrics for Generative AI
I
I. Business Metrics for Generative AI │ └── I1. Measuring Real-World Success ├── Technical performance alone ≠ AI success ├── Success = technical performance + business value (both required) ├── User Satisfaction — surveys / ratings: do users find output helpful and trustworthy? ├── ARPU — Average Revenue Per User: does AI personalization increase spend? ├── Cross-Domain Performance — FM versatility across multiple domains ├── Conversion Rate — % completing desired action (purchase, sign-up) ├── Efficiency — resource and time savings vs. baseline (operational ROI) └── Metrics are use-case specific — no universal success metric
Module Summary
  • Generative AI creates (text, visual, audio, code); ML analyzes/predicts. AI adds business value through automation, optimization, and personalization across 6 industries (Media, Retail, Healthcare, Life Sciences, Finance, Manufacturing).
  • Four application domains: CV (classify → detect → segment) · NLP (classify, sentiment, translate, generate) · IDP (extract, classify, summarize) · Fraud Detection (learn normal, flag deviations). AWS services: Rekognition · Comprehend/Translate/Lex · Textract.
  • Use ML for complex rules, high variability, large scale. Skip for simple deterministic logic. ML subtypes: Supervised (Classification · Regression) · Unsupervised (Clustering · Dimensionality Reduction) · Reinforcement (Agent → Environment → Reward/Penalty → Policy · AWS: DeepRacer).
  • 7 Gen AI capabilities: Adaptability · Responsiveness · Simplicity · Creativity · Data Efficiency · Personalization · Scalability. 7 risks: Hallucinations (plausible but false, primary exam risk) · Toxicity · Privacy · Regulatory · Social · Interpretability · Nondeterminism. Mitigate with: Guardrails · RAG · Curation · Testing · Monitoring.
  • Model selection: 5 factors (Type · Performance · Constraints · Compliance [hard gate] · Cost). 6 FMs: Jurassic-2 · Titan · Claude · Stable Diffusion · Cohere · Llama. Success = technical performance + business metrics (Satisfaction · ARPU · Conversion · Efficiency).
AWS-AI MasterClass · Module 02 · Exploring AI Use Cases and Applications
